name: Bayesian Cue-Belief Agent with Motivational Gating
description: A probabilistic Bayesian belief-updating agent that maintains posterior Beta distributions over cue-food contingencies rather than point-estimate associative weights. Each grid cell's cues (spatial features, nearby resource identifiers) have associated Beta(a_i, b_i) posteriors representing the agent's belief about the probability that cue i predicts food. A leaky decay factor (omega) on pseudo-counts implements recency weighting, naturally producing extinction and spontaneous recovery. The agent combines cue posteriors via noisy-OR to estimate food probability at each cell, then selects actions by maximizing expected utility that blends hunger-modulated exploitation with an epistemic exploration bonus proportional to posterior uncertainty. This is fundamentally different from the error-correction (Rescorla-Wagner) and temporal-difference approaches: it uses full distributions, principled uncertainty quantification, and information-seeking exploration.

variables.name: beta_alpha_params
variables.description: Dictionary mapping cue identifiers to the 'a' (success count) parameter of a Beta(a,b) posterior distribution over the cue's food-prediction probability.

variables.name: beta_beta_params
variables.description: Dictionary mapping cue identifiers to the 'b' (failure count) parameter of the Beta(a,b) posterior.

variables.name: posterior_mean
variables.description: Point estimate of food probability for cue i: theta_hat_i = a_i / (a_i + b_i)

variables.name: posterior_uncertainty
variables.description: Variance of the Beta posterior for cue i: U_i = a_i*b_i / ((a_i+b_i)^2 * (a_i+b_i+1)). Higher uncertainty drives exploration.

variables.name: food_probability
variables.description: Estimated probability of food at a cell, combined from all cue posteriors via noisy-OR: P_food(s) = 1 - prod(1 - theta_hat_i for i in cues(s))

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Internal hunger/drive state. Rises each step, drops upon eating. Gates the exploitation component of expected utility.

variables.name: expected_utility
variables.description: Expected utility of each candidate action, combining hunger-modulated food probability (exploitation) and uncertainty bonus (exploration).

parameters.name: prior_decay_rate
parameters.source: Nature Communications (2025); ensures non-stationarity adaptation via leaky Bayesian update

parameters.name: hunger_modulation_exponent
parameters.source: Researchgate (2017); motivational gating of appetitive responses

parameters.name: perception_radius
parameters.source: Grid-world constraint; agent evaluates cells within this Manhattan distance

rules.description: Cue identification: Each cell generates cues based on spatial coordinates and nearby food resource positions, similar to Formulation 1 but used for Bayesian posteriors.
rules.pseudocode: cues_at_cell = ['cell_' + str(sx) + '_' + str(sy)]; for f in perception.resources.food: if abs(f.x - sx) <= 1 and abs(f.y - sy) <= 1: cues_at_cell.append('food_near_' + str(f.x) + '_' + str(f.y))

rules.description: Leaky Bayesian posterior update: decay existing pseudo-counts then incorporate new observation. Decay applies to all known cues to implement recency weighting.
rules.pseudocode:
# Global decay on all tracked cues each step:
for cue in all_tracked_cues: a_i[cue] = omega * a_i[cue]; b_i[cue] = omega * b_i[cue]; a_i[cue] = max(a_i[cue], 0.01); b_i[cue] = max(b_i[cue], 0.01)
# Update cues at the cell where outcome was observed:
for cue in cues_at_outcome_cell: if ate_food: a_i[cue] = a_i.get(cue, a_0) + 1.0; else: b_i[cue] = b_i.get(cue, b_0) + 1.0

rules.description: Posterior mean: point estimate of food probability for each cue
Posterior uncertainty: variance of Beta distribution, drives exploration bonus
Food probability at a cell via noisy-OR combination of cue posteriors
rules.pseudocode: P_food_s = 1.0 - product((1.0 - theta_hat(cue)) for cue in cues_at_cell(s))

rules.description: Expected utility for movement/stay actions: exploitation (hunger-modulated food probability) plus exploration bonus (uncertainty)
rules.pseudocode: exploration_bonus = w_explore * max(sqrt(U_i(cue)) for cue in cues_at_cell(s_dest)) if cues_at_cell(s_dest) else 0.0; EU[action] = (H ** phi) * P_food(s_dest) + exploration_bonus

rules.description: Expected utility for eat action: only valid if food is perceived at current cell
rules.pseudocode: if food_at_current_cell: EU['eat'] = (H ** phi) * P_food(s_current) + (H ** phi); else: EU['eat'] = -999.0

rules.description: Hunger dynamics: identical hunger model across all formulations
rules.pseudocode: ate_flag = 1.0 if ate_food else 0.0; H = min(1.0, max(0.0, H + kappa - eta_sat * ate_flag))

rules.description: Greedy action selection: pick action with highest expected utility, break ties randomly
rules.pseudocode: a_star = argmax(EU); if multiple actions tied at max EU: a_star = random_choice(tied_actions); return a_star

decision_logic.description: Agent maintains Beta-distribution beliefs over cue-food contingencies. For each reachable cell, it computes food probability via noisy-OR over cue posteriors, then evaluates expected utility as a hunger-gated exploitation term plus an uncertainty-driven exploration bonus. Selects action with highest EU. After observing the outcome, it applies leaky Bayesian updates to all cues at the outcome cell.
pseudocode: # 1. Identify reachable cells and their cues
dest = compute_destination(action, perception.x, perception.y, grid_width, grid_height)
# Initialize any new cues with prior Beta(a_0, b_0)
if cue not in a_i: a_i[cue] = a_0; b_i[cue] = b_0
# Compute posterior means and food probability via noisy-OR
theta_hats = [a_i[c] / (a_i[c] + b_i[c]) for c in cues]
P_food_dest = 1.0 - product(1.0 - th for th in theta_hats) if theta_hats else 0.0
# Compute exploration bonus from max uncertainty
explore_bonus = w_explore * sqrt(max(uncertainties)) if uncertainties else 0.0
food_here = any(f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y)
best_actions = [a for a, eu in candidates.items() if abs(eu - max_eu) < 1e-6]

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_cell: [f for f in perception['resources']['food'] if f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y']]
perception_to_variables.ate_food: if action was 'eat' and reward > 0 then outcome o=1 else o=0

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward=1.0 when food present; used as binary outcome signal for Bayesian updates

expected_behaviors.description: Posterior convergence: after repeated food encounters at a location, Beta posteriors for cues at that location converge to high theta_hat (near 1.0) with low uncertainty
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps with stable food at (5,5) -> assert theta_hat for cues at (5,5) > 0.7 and U < 0.05

expected_behaviors.description: Leaky extinction: when food is removed, the decay factor omega causes pseudo-counts to shrink, naturally reverting the posterior toward the prior (theta_hat -> 0.5)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: train 100 steps with food, remove food, run 200 steps -> assert theta_hat for former food cues < 0.6

expected_behaviors.description: Exploration of uncertain cues: agent preferentially visits cells with high posterior variance (novel or ambiguous cues) even if mean food probability is moderate
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: introduce a new cue at a cell agent hasn't visited -> assert agent visits within 20 steps due to exploration bonus

expected_behaviors.description: Noisy-OR cue interaction: cells with multiple food-predicting cues have higher estimated food probability than cells with a single cue
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: cell A has 1 cue with theta_hat=0.5, cell B has 2 cues each theta_hat=0.5 -> assert P_food(B) > P_food(A)

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger gating: low hunger reduces expected utility of food-seeking, causing agent to explore more or stay in place
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: compare movement patterns at H=0.1 vs H=0.9 -> assert more exploration-driven actions at low H and more food-directed actions at high H

expected_behaviors.description: Spontaneous recovery analog: after extinction, the leaky prior slowly restores partial belief, so re-encountering a former food location causes rapid re-acquisition
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: train, extinguish, wait 100 steps, re-introduce food -> assert re-acquisition is faster than original acquisition

references: Ghirlanda, S. (2020) — A-Learning: A New Formulation of Associative Learning Theory — Psychonomic Bulletin & Review
Oxford / Cerebral Cortex (2008) — A Dual Role for Prediction Error in Associative Learning
Nature Communications (2025) — Reconciling Time and Prediction Error Theories of Associative Learning
Rescorla, R.A. & Wagner, A.R. (1972) — A Theory of Pavlovian Conditioning
Researchgate (2017) — Motivational State Controls the Prediction Error in Pavlovian Appetitive-Aversive Interactions
PMC3702630 (2013) — Extinction of Goal-Tracking Also Eliminates the Conditioned Appetitive Response
